overview.next_milestone: Extend runway with KfW-ready banking and accounting package

legal.reasoning: With an available capital of EUR 8,000, the UG (haftungsbeschränkt) is the most appropriate legal form, as it circumvents the EUR 25,000 minimum requirement of a standard GmbH. This structure provides the necessary liability shield for a B2B SaaS venture while preserving the founders' ability to self-fund their growth. The UG offers a professional, scalable foundation that can be converted into a full GmbH once the statutory reserves reach the required threshold, making it the ideal choice for a lean, Berlin-based startup.
entity_rationale: Min. capital can stay low while preserving a later GmbH conversion path.
Formation costs remain lower than a full GmbH setup.
The team can validate demand before locking in a larger capital structure.

incorporation_steps.title: IHK Namenscheck
incorporation_steps.description: Validate the company name for ertzuhjik with the local IHK before the notary draft is finalized.

incorporation_steps.title: Gesellschaftsvertrag
incorporation_steps.description: Prepare the articles of association and shareholder structure for the notary appointment.

incorporation_steps.title: Notartermin
incorporation_steps.description: Sign the incorporation documents and management appointment with a German notary.

incorporation_steps.title: Stammkapital
incorporation_steps.description: Open the business account and complete the Lean initial capital deposit transfer before Handelsregister filing.

incorporation_steps.title: Handelsregister
incorporation_steps.description: The notary files the company with the Amtsgericht and the entity becomes active after registration.

post_incorporation_checklist.title: Gewerbeanmeldung
post_incorporation_checklist.description: Register the trade locally in Berlin once Handelsregister entry is complete.

post_incorporation_checklist.title: Finanzamt registration
post_incorporation_checklist.description: Submit the tax registration questionnaire and request the Steuernummer and USt-IdNr.

post_incorporation_checklist.title: Business banking live
post_incorporation_checklist.description: Finalize cards, accounting connections, and expense flows after the capital deposit clears.

post_incorporation_checklist.title: Compliance pages
post_incorporation_checklist.description: Publish Impressum, Datenschutzerklaerung, and cookie consent before public launch.

legal.narrative: ertzuhjik is positioned to begin its legal formation in Berlin using the UG (haftungsbeschränkt) framework. This path is specifically designed for capital-efficient bootstrapping, allowing the founders to secure liability protection while maintaining a lean financial profile. The incorporation sequence moves from a preliminary IHK name validation to the formal notarization of the articles of association and the mandatory capital deposit. Upon completion of the Handelsregister entry—estimated to take approximately four weeks—the company will be fully operational and ready to execute its B2B SaaS strategy.
legal.conversion_note: Retain 25% of annual profits until the company reaches the EUR 25,000 level typically associated with a later GmbH conversion.

evidence.title: GmbHG and UG capital structure rules
evidence.rationale: Anchors the UG versus GmbH recommendation in the German limited-liability company framework.

evidence.title: IHK name-check and registration guidance
evidence.rationale: Supports the sequence around name validation, notary preparation, and register filing.

scenarios.label: conservative
scenarios.note: Adds hiring and tooling sooner, compressing runway.

scenarios.label: base
scenarios.note: Balanced path using the current plan and timing.

scenarios.label: optimistic
scenarios.note: Assumes a leaner team ramp and tighter vendor spend.

tax_notes: Gewerbesteuer in the selected Bundesland is modeled at about 14%.
Employer social cost is modeled with a 20% uplift on gross salary.
Umsatzsteuer is usually 19%, with different handling if the company remains very small.

funding_programs.name: EXIST-Gruenderstipendium
funding_programs.summary: Best fit for university-linked founders who need non-dilutive support before fundraising.

funding_programs.name: KfW StartGeld
funding_programs.summary: Debt capital for young German companies that need early operating runway.

funding_programs.name: EIC Accelerator
funding_programs.summary: Relevant for deep-tech companies that can support an EU-scale innovation case.

funding_programs.name: HTGF readiness
funding_programs.summary: Signal that the company should prepare investor-facing materials for a German pre-seed process.

assumptions: Employer social security contributions (Arbeitgeberanteil) are modeled at a 20% uplift on gross salaries.
Runway is calculated strictly on current cash reserves and does not factor in speculative B2B revenue or sales cycles.
The Berlin-specific trade tax (Gewerbesteuer) is estimated at approximately 14% based on the local multiplier (Hebelsatz).
EXIST-Gründerstipendium eligibility assumes the founders maintain an active university link and meet the formal application criteria.
Operating expenses include essential SaaS infrastructure, legal formation costs for a German entity, and basic B2B go-to-market activities.
finance.narrative: With a monthly burn of EUR 8,400 against EUR 8,000 in available capital, your runway is critical and effectively stands at 1 month. Although your primary goal is to bootstrap, the current liquidity gap requires immediate action to sustain operations. We recommend a target raise of EUR 90,000 in month 1 to bridge this gap. Given your university affiliation, prioritizing the EXIST-Gründerstipendium is the most strategic path to secure non-dilutive funding while maintaining your long-term self-funding objectives.

evidence.title: EXIST Gruenderstipendium programme rules
evidence.rationale: Supports non-dilutive funding eligibility logic for university-linked founders.

evidence.title: KfW StartGeld overview
evidence.rationale: Supports early debt-financing availability for young German companies.

evidence.title: Bundesland Gewerbesteuer assumptions
evidence.rationale: Documents the location-based trade-tax rate used in the runway model.

hiring.recommendation: Maintain a strictly founder-led operation until formal incorporation is finalized. Given the limited initial capital, defer all full-time employment contracts. Post-incorporation, prioritize high-leverage, low-cost support—specifically Werkstudierende—to preserve runway while founders focus on customer discovery and core development.

first_roles.title: Founder complement or advisor
first_roles.rationale: Cover legal or finance blind spots without committing to payroll before incorporation.

first_roles.title: Werkstudent operations support
first_roles.rationale: Low-cost support for research, admin, and founder enablement.

employment_law_flags.title: Probezeit
employment_law_flags.description: A probation period of up to 6 months is standard once full-time hiring starts.

employment_law_flags.title: Urlaubsanspruch
employment_law_flags.description: The statutory minimum is 20 paid vacation days on a 5-day week.

milestones: Finish incorporation before adding full-time payroll.
Use advisors or students for targeted support only.
Keep founder capacity focused on validation and not bureaucracy.
hiring.narrative: The current setup reflects a classic pre-seed bootstrapping scenario in Berlin's B2B SaaS sector. With €8,000 in available capital, the primary objective is to reach a 'default alive' state through extreme burn-rate discipline. The founders' university affiliation provides a vital pipeline for student talent, which is the most risk-mitigated way to scale operations in Germany. This strategy emphasizes legal compliance and financial runway over aggressive headcount growth, ensuring the venture remains agile during the critical validation phase.

evidence.title: Werkstudent and probation norms
evidence.rationale: Explains why the first hiring plan favors student leverage and probationary caution.

evidence.title: Dismissal protection thresholds
evidence.rationale: Supports the warning around stricter employment protections as headcount rises.

dsgvo_checklist.title: Datenschutzerklaerung
dsgvo_checklist.description: Publish a German-compliant privacy policy across the website and the product.

dsgvo_checklist.title: Impressum
dsgvo_checklist.description: Every public-facing German website needs a valid legal notice before launch.

dsgvo_checklist.title: Cookie consent
dsgvo_checklist.description: Configure opt-in consent for analytics and marketing cookies, not pre-checked banners.

dsgvo_checklist.title: AVV with processors
dsgvo_checklist.description: Sign data-processing agreements with each SaaS vendor that sees personal data.

dsgvo_checklist.title: Processing register
dsgvo_checklist.description: Maintain a Verzeichnis von Verarbeitungstaetigkeiten for core product and recruiting workflows.

dsgvo_checklist.title: DPO threshold
dsgvo_checklist.description: Flag the Datenschutzbeauftragter requirement if more than 20 people process personal data regularly.

tool_stack.why: Steuerberater-ready bookkeeping backbone for German reporting.
tool_stack.compliance_note: Use DATEV export or direct integration from day one.

tool_stack.why: Simple invoicing and bookkeeping for early-stage teams.
tool_stack.compliance_note: Good bridge until the tax advisor wants fuller DATEV workflows.

tool_stack.why: Fast business banking with a German IBAN and clean team controls.
tool_stack.compliance_note: Suitable for Stammkapital deposit and day-one operations.

tool_stack.why: Hiring, contracts, and payroll operations in one place.
tool_stack.compliance_note: Pair with local legal review for contract templates.

tool_stack.why: Widely used cookie consent platform with Germany-first posture.
tool_stack.compliance_note: Configure opt-in by default for non-essential cookies.

required_setups: EU-hosted backend and documented processor list
German IBAN business bank account for incorporation and operations
Accounting workflow that can export into DATEV
Published Impressum and privacy pages before launch
ops.e_invoicing_note: In accordance with the German Growth Opportunities Act (Wachstumschancengesetz), all B2B entities must be capable of receiving electronic invoices (E-Rechnungen) starting January 1, 2025. ertzuhjik should ensure its accounting stack natively supports structured formats like ZUGFeRD or XRechnung to remain compliant with these mandatory B2B invoicing standards from day one.
compliance_highlights: Germany expects opt-in cookie consent for non-essential tracking.
A DATEV-compatible accounting flow prevents pain with local tax advisors later.
B2B startups should be ready to receive e-invoices under the German 2025 regime.
ops.narrative: For a Berlin-based B2B SaaS like ertzuhjik, operational excellence starts with a 'compliance-by-design' approach. Given the lean €8,000 capital base, prioritizing a DATEV-compatible accounting workflow and robust DSGVO foundations—including a compliant Impressum and data processing agreements (AVVs)—is essential to avoid costly retroactive fixes. Treating these regulatory requirements as core infrastructure rather than administrative overhead will ensure the company remains professional and audit-ready throughout its self-funded growth phase.

evidence.title: DSGVO controller and processor obligations
evidence.rationale: Supports the privacy checklist, AVV requirements, and processor inventory.

evidence.title: German B2B e-invoicing rollout
evidence.rationale: Supports the recommendation to choose accounting tools that can receive compliant e-invoices.
